# UC-22.2.42 ┬Ę NIS2 Board-Level Cyber Risk Reporting Distribution Audit

## Description

Reconciles each `report_name` from `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` against `index=_audit` scheduler events and highlights board cyber packs whose PDF/email dispatch is absent or older than the policy cadence encoded in `frequency`.

## Value

Gives the board secretary and incident leadership an automatic paper trail that scheduled cyber risk briefings actually left Splunk on time, which matters when regulators ask whether oversight artefacts reached directorsŌĆönot merely whether dashboards exist.

## Implementation

(1) Keep `savedsearch_name` identical to `report_name` (case-insensitive) or maintain a mapping lookup. (2) Ensure alert actions log `sendemail` with `format=pdf` where possible so `info` matches the SPL predicate. (3) Delegate ownership to Head of IR with secretariat co-ownership documented in the runbook. (4) Weekly schedule with **180-day** lookback. (5) Archive positive outcomes to `index=audit_evidence` if your programme requires WORM storage beyond `_audit` retention.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Splunk administrators must confirm `auditTrail` (often materialised as `index=_audit` with `sourcetype=audittrail`) is retained for at least **180 days** on indexers ŌĆö Cloud defaults usually exceed this; Enterprise teams should verify `indexes.conf` frozenTimePeriodInSecs.
ŌĆó Roles running this search need `indexes=_audit` and `search`/`schedule_search` capabilities; delegate via a named role `board_report_auditor` rather than sharing `admin` passwords.
ŌĆó Populate `nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/<governance_app>/lookups/` and register it in `transforms.conf`; columns must include `report_name`, `frequency`, `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date` for secretariat cross-checks.
ŌĆó Hardware clock sync: skew >5 minutes between search heads and indexers makes `days_since` misleading ŌĆö validate NTP before go-live.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Standardise Splunk report names in **Settings ŌåÆ Searches, reports, and alerts ŌåÆ Reports** so each board pack uses a `savedsearch_name` that normalises cleanly to the lookup (`lower()` match in the SPL). Enable **Schedule** with PDF output and email action; confirm `default.meta` exports the object to the `search` app if multiple teams edit it.

Optional hardening: pipe alerts through a heavy forwarder with `outputs.conf` client certs ŌĆö not required for `_audit`, but keeps mail actions observable in hybrid layouts.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search
Clone the SPL into `UC-22.2.42 ŌĆö Board report distribution audit`. Schedule weekly (Monday 06:00) with summary indexing disabled ŌĆö you want interactive review for missed cadences.

Understanding this SPL: the lookup lists the should-have-sent board packs; the left join keeps catalogue entries even if `_audit` never recorded completion. `match(info,...)` focuses on PDF/email dispatch markers Splunk writes into `info`. `expected_cadence_days` encodes **35-day** monthly tolerance (covers five-week months) and **100-day** quarterly tolerance. `evidence_id` fingerprints each miss for GRC exports.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Pick a known-good report from last month: run the subsearch alone (`index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail action=search savedsearch_name="<name>"`) and confirm `user` matches the service account. Compare `last_delivery` to the PDF stored in the board portal. Run `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches` locally to confirm the cron matches `frequency`.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Dashboard layout: row 1 ŌĆö count of open misses; row 2 ŌĆö table grouped by `frequency`; row 3 ŌĆö timeline. Alert via email to Head of IR and board secretary distro. Attach runbook describing how to replay the saved search with `sendpreview` after remediation.

Step 5 ŌĆö Troubleshooting
ŌĆó **Zero `_audit` rows** ŌĆö A role removed `dispatch.auto_*` permissions; check `splunkd.log` for `AuditLogger` errors or verify the search actually ran (Settings ŌåÆ Activity ŌåÆ Triggered alerts).
ŌĆó **Everything flagged missed despite PDFs arriving** ŌĆö `savedsearch_name` mismatch; reconcile using `| inputlookup nis2_board_report_schedule.csv` against `| metadata type=savedsearches`.
ŌĆó **info field lacks sendemail text on Splunk Cloud** ŌĆö Some builds abbreviate `info`; broaden the regex or add `OR match(info,"dashboard")` temporarily while Splunk Support reviews your tenantŌĆÖs audit format.
ŌĆó **Join latency** ŌĆö Subsearch hits indexer quota; shrink earliest to **120d** but document reduced forensic depth.
ŌĆó **False stale during indexer maintenance** ŌĆö Maintenance windows should be recorded; use `lookup maintenance_window host OUTPUT paused | where paused!=1` if you add host metadata later.

Command-line aids: `splunk btool savedsearches list --debug | grep sendemail` confirms mail actions at the CLI without opening the UI.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_board_report_schedule.csv
| eval report_key=lower(report_name)
| join type=left report_key [
    search index=_audit sourcetype=audittrail action=search earliest=-180d
    | where match(info,"(?i)sendemail|pdf|rendered")
    | eval report_key=lower(savedsearch_name)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_delivery by report_key
]
| eval expected_cadence_days=case(frequency="monthly",35,frequency="quarterly",100,frequency="weekly",10,1=1,14)
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_delivery), round((now()-last_delivery)/86400,0), null())
| eval missed_cadence=if(isnull(last_delivery),1,if(days_since>expected_cadence_days,1,0))
| where missed_cadence=1
| eval evidence_id=md5(report_name . "|" . frequency . "|" . if(isnotnull(last_delivery), tostring(last_delivery), "no_audit_hit"))
| table report_name, frequency, distribution_list, last_expected_date, last_delivery, days_since, expected_cadence_days, evidence_id
```

## Visualization

Table of missed rows with `distribution_list`, `last_expected_date`, and `evidence_id`; timeline of `last_delivery`; single value count of breaches by `frequency`.
